<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum number of substance abuse incidents occurs at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents.
To keep incidents below 20, the community should maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.37.
This human effort to control social issues is as vital as water to a baseball team in the western region.
The calculated range ensures the community stays within safe limits for public health.
Strict adherence to these economic targets will help prevent the rise in substance abuse.
Remember that the model applies only within the defined stress index range of 0 to 5.
